Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that filters out cases with more than 6 events from an event log table named event_log The table has columns case_id activity timestamp We need to count the number of events per case_id exclude those where the count greater than 6 and return all events for the remaining case_ids

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a classic filtering based on an aggregate condition We can use a window function or a subquery with HAVING and a JOIN or IN clause

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Using window function SELECT FROM SELECT COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id AS event_count FROM event_log WHERE event_count less than or equal to 6

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Using IN clause SELECT FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT less than or equal to 6

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Both are valid in DuckDB The window function approach is often quite efficient and concise Lets provide the window function approach as the primary answer but mention the HAVING approach as well

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait the prompt says Please craft a DuckDB SQL query Let me write a clean query

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Window function approach SELECT case_id activity timestamp FROM SELECT COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id AS event_count FROM event_log subquery WHERE event_count less than or equal to 6

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Subquery with HAVING approach SELECT e case_id e activity e timestamp FROM event_log e JOIN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT less than or equal to 6 c ON e case_id equals c case_id

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Or using IN SELECT case_id activity timestamp FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT less than or equal to 6

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets just give the window function query as its very idiomatic for DuckDB and doesnt require a join or IN Also I can select all to return all columns

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait if we use SELECT all the column case_event_count will also be returned The prompt says returns only the events from cases that have six or fewer events in total Its usually fine but to strictly return just the event columns we can drop the count column DuckDB supports SELECT all EXCLUDE case_event_count or we can just do it in the subquery with a WHERE EXISTS or IN

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets use the IN or JOIN approach to avoid adding an extra column or use EXCLUDE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: SELECT all EXCLUDE case_event_count FROM SELECT COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id AS case_event_count FROM event_log WHERE case_event_count less than or equal to 6

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Actually using IN is very clean and doesnt add extra columns SELECT all FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT less than or equal to 6
